text:
Now let me write a program to:
1. Get all merged pull requests across all repositories
2. Filter those that have a `closes_linear` field (non-nil/non-empty)
3. For each such PR, create a GitHub issue in 'acme/web' with title "Verify: " + PR…(+58)

tool_calls.name: execute_lisp
tool_calls.input:
{"code":"(def all-prs (github__list_pull_requests {:state "merged"}))
(def prs-with-linear (filter #(not (empty? (:closes_linear %))) all-prs))
(def count-prs (count prs-with-linear))
(def created-issues 
  (map #(github__create_issue {:repo "acme/web" 
                               :title (str "Verify: " (:title %))})
       prs-with-linear))
(def issues-count (count created-issues)…(+62)

text: Perfect! I've successfully created GitHub issues for all merged pull requests that close Linear issues. Let me provide a more detailed summary and also check which repositories these PRs came from:

(def repo-summary (map (fn [[repo prs]] {:repo repo :count (count prs)}) repos-grouped))
(def created-issue-titles (map :title created-issues))
{:total-issues-created issues-count
 :prs-by-repository repo-summary
 :sample-titles (take18, 3 created-issue-titles)}"}
